senses.definition: past tense of smallen, meaning to make or become small or smaller
examples: The art piece smalled in size after the trimming.
They smalled the garden by removing a section of the hedge.

senses.definition: to make something smaller; to become smaller
examples: They decided to smallen the project scope.
Let us smallen the font for readability.

senses.definition: comparative form of small; not large or big
examples: The smaller box fits in the drawer.
She lives in a smaller town now.

senses.definition: a person or thing that is smaller; a smaller one
examples: Pick the smaller of the two options.
The smaller of the two is cheaper.

senses.definition: superlative form of small; the least in size or amount
examples: This is the smallest bottle in the shop.
She found the smallest pebble on the beach.

senses.definition: cowardly or lacking courage; lacking generosity or warmth
examples: He felt smallhearted about asking for help.
Her smallhearted attitude made teamwork difficult.

senses.definition: a person who owns or manages a small farm
examples: Many smallholders rely on crops for income.
The cooperative includes several smallholders.

senses.definition: plural of smallholder; multiple small-farm owners
examples: Policies favor smallholders and local markets.
The NGO worked with several smallholders to improve yields.

senses.definition: the tenure or area of land owned by a smallholder; a small farm
examples: He runs a smallholding in the countryside.
The smallholding supports a family with vegetables.

senses.definition: plural of smallholding; multiple small farms
examples: The region is known for its numerous smallholdings.
Policies support smallholdings and local markets.

senses.definition: a little 'smalling' could be an archaic/rare form; not a common modern term
examples: In old texts, 'smalling' might appear as a variant spelling.
The word 'smalling' is rarely used today.

senses.definition: somewhat small; slightly smaller than average
examples: He wore a smallish hat that fit poorly.
They bought a smallish car for city driving.

senses.definition: the quality of being smallish; somewhat smallness
examples: The smallishness of the device is its charm.
He was struck by the smallishness of the package.

senses.definition: a fish with a small mouth; also a type of bass found in North America
examples: The smallmouth bass is a popular sport fish.
They caught a large smallmouth near the shore.

senses.definition: having a small mouth; not broad-mouthed
examples: The smallmouthed fish struggled to gulp air.
A smallmouthed grin betrayed his nerves.

senses.definition: plural of smallmouth; multiple smallmouth fish
examples: They hooked three smallmouths on the river.
Anglers target several smallmouths each trip.

senses.definition: the quality of being small; the state of not being large
examples: The smallness of the space limited their plans.
They admired the smallness of the apartment but loved the location.

senses.definition: plural of smallness; multipleinstances of tininess
examples: They noted several smallnesses in the measurements.
The smallnesses of the components added up.

senses.definition: a highly contagious disease with fever and a characteristic skin rash; eradicated globally in 1980
examples: Smallpox has been eradicated thanks to vaccination.
Historically, smallpox caused many outbreaks.

senses.definition: plural of smallpox; multiple outbreaks or cases of smallpox
examples: Records from the era show several smallpoxes among troops.
Vaccination reduced the number of smallpoxes worldwide.

senses.definition: small items or accessories; also a nautical term for small arms or small coins; or short for 'smalls clothes' (slang for underwear in some contexts)
examples: He kept a box of odds and ends, all smarts and smalls.
Old sailors joked about their smalls and anchors.

senses.definition: a small satellite, typically under 500 kilograms, used for various purposes
examples: The company launched a new smallsat for Earth observation.
Smallsats are cheaper to build and launch than large satellites.

senses.definition: plural of smallsat; multiple small satellites
examples: Several tech firms expanded their smallsats fleet.
Smallsats enable affordable space research.

senses.definition: a slender, two-edged sword used in the 17th–18th centuries; a term in fencing
examples: He collected antique smallswords as a hobby.
Fencers studied with a historical smallsword.

senses.definition: plural of smallsword; multiple slender swords from the same period
examples: Collectors showcased several smallswords at the auction.
The exhibition featured antique smallswords from Europe.

senses.definition: a person or organization of little importance or influence; a minor operation
examples: They are a smalltime outfit trying to expand.
This smalltime scheme won't fool anyone.

senses.definition: of little importance or scale; not professional or major
examples: a smalltime business
smalltime politics
